fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Kansas Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as an occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Kansas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity kansas, how to transfer occupational therapist license to kansas, occupational therapist compact kansas, kansas occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Kansas Board of Healing Arts — OT
board.parent_dept: Kansas Department for Aging and Disability Services (KDADS)
board.mailing_address: 800 SW Jackson, Lower Level – Suite A, Topeka, KS 66612

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Kansas.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Register for an Online Account
steps.description: Visit the KBHA Online Portal at kbha.kansas.gov/onlineportal/ and click 'Register' to create a new user account if you do not already have one.

steps.title: Start the Occupational Therapist Application
steps.description: Log in to your newly created or existing KBHA Online Portal account, navigate to the 'Application' section, and select the Occupational Therapist application.

steps.title: Complete Online Application and Upload Documents
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application, including personal, educational, and professional history. Upload any necessary supporting documents directly through the KBHA Online Portal.

steps.title: Arrange for Official Documents Submission
steps.description: Ensure your official transcripts are sent directly from your educational institution and your NBCOT score verification is sent directly from NBCOT to the Kansas Board of Healing Arts.

steps.title: Complete the Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Access and successfully complete the Kansas Jurisprudence Exam, which is a mandatory component of the application process, available through the KBHA Online Portal.

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $275.00 through the KBHA Online Portal to finalize your application submission.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Kansas offers a temporary occupational therapy permit for new graduates who have completed an ACOTE-accredited program and are awaiting their NBCOT exam results. The temporary permit allows practice under the direct supervision of a licensed OTR. Contact the Kansas Board of Healing Arts — OT for current temporary permit requirements and duration limits.
reciprocity_text: Kansas is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Kansas without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Kansas also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

faqs.question: How much does a Kansas OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial application and licensing fee is approximately $100. Check the board website for the latest fee schedule.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Kansas have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Kansas has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Kansas?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Kansas while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Kansas is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Kansas without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Kansas also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

hero.title: Become an Occupational Therapist in Kansas
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in Kansas, applicants must submit their application through the KBHA Online Portal.

variants.label: New Applicant

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: To create an account, visit the KBHA Online Portal and click the 'Register' link. Complete the required registration form, verify your email address, and then log in. Once logged in, select 'Application' from the main menu to begin your application.
